data.title: Drax Continues Share Buyback, Lifts Treasury Holdings to Nearly 96 Million Shares
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Drax Group plc ( (GB:DRX) ).

Drax Group plc has continued executing its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 73,070 ordinary shares on 31 March 2026 at prices between 876.0p and 886.5p, with a volume-weighted average price of 882.3632p. Following this latest transaction, the company has 432,461,089 shares in issue, of which 96,027,685 are held in treasury, leaving 336,433,404 voting rights outstanding for shareholders’ disclosure calculations.

Since the launch of the buyback in July 2025, Drax has acquired a total of 8,934,873 shares for treasury, signalling an ongoing commitment to returning capital to shareholders and actively managing its capital structure. The detailed trade data disclosed, including venue-level aggregation, also underscores the company’s adherence to market abuse regulations and transparency requirements for investors and regulators.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:DRX) stock is a Sell with a £720.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Drax Group plc stock, see the GB:DRX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DRX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DRX is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mid-range financial performance (strong cash generation and manageable leverage but weaker recent profitability) and supportive technicals (trading above key moving averages with neutral momentum). Valuation metrics are very favorable on the provided P/E and dividend yield, while the earnings call adds a positive tilt from reiterated multi-year free cash flow and shareholder returns, tempered by impairments and near-term earnings pressure under the new CfD regime.

To see Spark’s full report on DRX stock, click here.

More about Drax Group plc

Drax Group plc is a UK-listed energy company focused on electricity generation and related services. It operates primarily through large-scale power assets and participates actively in UK capital markets, with its ordinary shares traded on the London Stock Exchange.

Average Trading Volume: 1,200,455

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £2.94B

data.title: Pearson Cancels 551,850 Shares in Ongoing £350m Buyback
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pearson ( (GB:PSON) ) has issued an update.

Pearson plc has repurchased 551,850 of its ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange at an average price of 991.01 pence per share, with prices ranging between 983.00 pence and 1,000.00 pence. The company plans to cancel these shares, reducing the overall share count.

This transaction forms part of the second £175 million tranche of Pearson’s ongoing £350 million share buyback programme, executed via Morgan Stanley & Co. International. The move underscores continued capital returns to shareholders and may enhance earnings per share by lowering the number of shares in circulation.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PSON) stock is a Hold with a £10.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pearson stock, see the GB:PSON Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PSON Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PSON is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by steady but not accelerating fundamentals—strong operating profitability offset by low growth, weaker net margin, rising leverage, and softer 2025 free cash flow. Earnings call guidance adds support via clear mid-single-digit growth and profit/cash-conversion targets, while technicals remain a headwind with the stock below key longer-term moving averages and negative MACD. Valuation is reasonable with a mid-teens/high-teens P/E and a ~2.5% dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on PSON stock, click here.

More about Pearson

Pearson plc is a global education and publishing company listed on the London Stock Exchange. It provides educational content, digital learning platforms and assessment services to schools, universities and professional learners, with a focus on technology-enabled education markets worldwide.

Aptitude Software Group plc ( (GB:APTD) ) has shared an announcement.

Aptitude Software Group plc has repurchased 10,000 ordinary shares at 220 pence each on the London Stock Exchange as part of its ongoing share buyback programme, with the shares to be held in treasury. Following this transaction, Aptitude now holds 695,000 shares in treasury and its total number of ordinary shares in issue, excluding treasury shares, stands at 54,551,064, a figure shareholders can use to assess disclosure thresholds under FCA transparency rules.

The latest buyback incrementally reduces the company’s free float, which can support earnings per share metrics and signals continued management confidence in the business. Aptitude plans to continue announcing further transactions under the buyback programme, underscoring an active capital management strategy that may be closely watched by investors and regulators alike.

data.title: Smiths Group completes £1.3bn Interconnect sale and launches major buyback
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Smiths Group plc ( (GB:SMIN) ) has provided an announcement.

Smiths Group has completed the sale of its Smiths Interconnect division to Molex Electronic Technologies Holdings, a Koch company, following an agreement first announced in October 2025. The move sharpens Smiths’ profile as a high-performance industrial engineering group focused on core markets, while shifting the Interconnect business to an owner more squarely rooted in electronic technologies.

The transaction generates about £1.3 billion in cash proceeds, of which £1 billion is being returned to shareholders via an ongoing share buyback programme. The remaining funds will be deployed into value-creative growth opportunities and used to strengthen Smiths’ balance sheet, underlining management’s intent to unlock capital from non-core assets and reinvest in higher-return areas for long-term stakeholder value.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SMIN) stock is a Hold with a £2474.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Smiths Group plc stock, see the GB:SMIN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SMIN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SMIN is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid profitability, balance sheet stability, and strong cash conversion, reinforced by a positive earnings outlook and shareholder returns. These strengths are tempered by weak technical momentum (downtrend and bearish MACD) and a relatively high P/E multiple despite a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SMIN stock, click here.

More about Smiths Group plc

Smiths Group is a London-listed industrial engineering company with a 175-year history and global operations in more than 50 countries. It focuses on engineering solutions for energy, industrials, construction and aerospace markets, addressing global needs such as decarbonisation and rising demand for process and energy efficiency.

Average Trading Volume: 1,432,753

data.title: One Health Group strengthens board with new CFO ahead of surgical hub expansion
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

One Health Group PLC ( (GB:OHGR) ) has provided an announcement.

One Health Group has appointed experienced chartered accountant Heather Craven as part-time chief financial officer and board member from 8 April 2026, bringing extensive governance, risk and restructuring expertise from FTSE, AIM and NHS roles. Her background includes senior positions at Rotherham NHS Foundation Trust, where she helped drive strategic change and strengthen oversight, experience the company expects to leverage as it prepares to open its first surgical hub in 2027.

The Group also announced that chief medical officer Shantanu Shahane will retire from his executive role to focus on clinical practice within the business, with Professor Anil Hormis joining as associate medical director to oversee an orderly handover. These board and leadership changes signal a strengthening of financial and clinical management as One Health enters a planned expansion phase, with implications for its operational capacity and positioning within the NHS-funded elective surgery market.

More about One Health Group PLC

One Health Group plc is an independent provider of NHS-funded surgical procedures in England, operating via GP referrals under the Patient Choice framework. The company works with around 80 NHS consultants and over 100 professionals across nine independent hospitals and 37 outreach clinics, focusing on orthopaedics, spine, general surgery, gynaecology and newly added urology in regions such as Yorkshire and the East Midlands.

Its model targets areas where local NHS services are under-supplied and private insurance uptake is low, deriving revenue from more than 60 NHS commissioning bodies and hospital trusts. One Health delivered care to 17,020 new patients in the year to March 2025, conducting over 42,000 consultations and 7,000 surgical procedures, with common operations including knee and hip replacements.

The Group emphasises community-based outreach clinics to minimise patient travel by delivering consultations and post-operative physiotherapy closer to home. Management sees strong structural demand, particularly in spine and orthopaedics, driven by ageing demographics, physical inactivity and rising obesity rates, underpinning its growth strategy and capacity expansion plans.

Average Trading Volume: 11,783

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Learn more about OHGR stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

Vertu Motors ( (GB:VTU) ) has provided an announcement.

Vertu Motors’ employee benefit trust, managed by Ocorian Limited, has acquired 107,400 ordinary shares at 58.49 pence each on the London Stock Exchange’s AIM market. This purchase increases the trust’s holding from 4,541,388 to 4,648,788 shares, representing a rise from 1.45% to 1.49% of the company’s issued share capital.

The shares will be held within the employee benefit trust to satisfy future awards under the company’s share-based incentive schemes, including its Long Term Incentive Plan for executive directors and other senior managers. The transaction underlines Vertu Motors’ ongoing use of equity-based remuneration to align management and employee interests with those of shareholders without altering the overall number of shares in issue.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:VTU) stock is a Buy with a £71.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vertu Motors stock, see the GB:VTU Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VTU Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VTU is a Outperform.

Vertu Motors’ overall stock score is driven by a stable financial foundation with solid revenue growth, supported by positive technical trends and a reasonable valuation. The strategic share buyback program further enhances shareholder value. However, profitability pressures and operational challenges, such as the cyber-attack, present risks that temper the score.

To see Spark’s full report on VTU stock, click here.

More about Vertu Motors

Vertu Motors plc is a UK-based automotive retailer operating franchised motor dealerships, primarily focused on selling new and used vehicles, as well as providing associated aftersales services. The company is listed on AIM of the London Stock Exchange and has a broad shareholder base, with all ordinary shares carrying voting rights and none held in treasury.

IG Group Holdings ( (GB:IGG) ) has issued an update.

IG Group Holdings, the London-listed financial technology group behind the IG, tastytrade, Freetrade and Independent Reserve brands, focuses on providing leveraged trading, stock and investment services, and cryptocurrency trading to a global retail client base. Its business model combines proprietary trading platforms with a multi-brand strategy to capture demand from active traders and investors across major markets.

The company has launched a new share buyback programme of up to £125 million, to be executed by Morgan Stanley in two tranches of up to £62.5 million each, with the first running from 1 April to an expected completion by 30 September 2026. The buyback, aimed solely at reducing share capital with repurchased shares to be held in treasury, signals continued capital strength and is likely to support earnings per share and shareholder returns, while leaving scope for a second tranche dependent on share price performance and other capital needs.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:IGG) stock is a Buy with a £1630.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IG Group Holdings stock, see the GB:IGG Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Mercury NZ ( (MGHTF) ).

Mercury NZ Limited has announced the issuance of 10,427,857 ordinary shares under its Dividend Reinvestment Plan (DRP) for the FY2025 final dividend. This issuance, which represents 0.74% of the total class of financial products, reflects Mercury’s ongoing commitment to providing value to its shareholders through reinvestment opportunities. The new shares are fully paid and rank equally with existing ordinary shares, potentially enhancing shareholder value and reinforcing Mercury’s position in the renewable energy sector.

It also provides retail services for electricity, gas, broadband, and mobile services. Mercury is listed on both the New Zealand Stock Exchange and the Australian Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ‘MCY’, with the New Zealand Government holding a minimum 51% shareholding.
